visualSignals: Find x-values where derivative is zero or undefined
nonExamples: Find maximum/minimum (need to test critical points)

decisionTree.question: Where is f'(x) = 0 or f'(x) undefined?
decisionTree.ifYes: These are critical points (if in domain of f)
decisionTree.ifNo: Function may have no critical points

steps.pitfall: Critical points must be in the domain of the original function
Differentiation errors will give wrong critical points
Don't forget to check where derivative DNE
steps.check: Look for division by zero, undefined trig values, etc.

steps.instruction: Check that critical points are in domain of f
steps.pitfall: Exclude points not in domain of original function
steps.check: Verify x-values are valid for f(x)

commonErrors.symptom: Extra critical points not in domain
commonErrors.diagnosis: Included points where f'(x) DNE but f(x) also DNE
commonErrors.fix: Critical points must be in domain of original function f
commonErrors.example: f(x) = 1/x has f'(x) = -1/x², undefined at x=0, but x=0 not in domain of f

commonErrors.diagnosis: Forgot to check where f'(x) is undefined
commonErrors.fix: Critical points occur where f'(x) = 0 OR f'(x) DNE
commonErrors.example: f(x) = |x| has critical point at x=0 where derivative DNE

commonErrors.symptom: Wrong critical points from algebra errors

workedExamples.problem: Find the critical points of f(x) = x³ - 6x² + 9x

solution.explanation: Critical points are x = 1 and x = 3

solution.math: f'(x) \text{ defined everywhere, so critical points are } \boxed{x = 1, 3}
solution.explanation: Check where derivative undefined (nowhere for polynomials)

workedExamples.keyInsight: For polynomials, critical points are where f'(x) = 0 since derivative always exists

workedExamples.problem: Find the critical points of f(x) = x^(2/3)

solution.explanation: Cube root defined for all x

solution.math: x = 0 \text{ is in domain of } f, \text{ so critical point is } \boxed{x = 0}

workedExamples.keyInsight: Critical points can occur where derivative is undefined (cusp at origin for x^(2/3))

workedExamples.problem: Find the critical points of f(x) = x²e^(-x)

solution.math: e^{-x} \neq 0 \text{ for all } x, \text{ so } 2x - x^2 = 0
f'(x) \text{ defined everywhere, so critical points are } \boxed{x = 0, 2}

workedExamples.keyInsight: Exponential functions are never zero, so focus on the polynomial factor

questions.integral: Find where f(x) = x³ - 3x has horizontal tangents
questions.explanation: Horizontal tangents occur where f'(x) = 0 - that's critical points

questions.integral: Where does f(x) = |x-2| have a corner?
questions.explanation: Corner means derivative undefined - that's a critical point

questions.integral: Find where f'(x) = 0 for f(x) = sin(x)
questions.explanation: f'(x) = 0 gives critical points (which include max and min)

problems.problem: Find critical points of f(x) = x² - 4x + 3
Find critical points of f(x) = x^(1/3)
Find critical points of f(x) = x²/(x-1)
problems.hint: Use quotient rule. f'(x) = (2x(x-1) - x²)/(x-1)² = (x²-2x)/(x-1)² = x(x-2)/(x-1)²

problems.problem: Find all critical points of f(x) = x - 2sin(x) on [0, 2π]

problems.context: Critical points with chain rule and undefined derivative

cheatSheet.oneLiner: Critical points: where f'(x) = 0 or f'(x) DNE (and x is in domain of f)
cheatSheet.formula: \text{Critical number } c: f'(c) = 0 \text{ or } f'(c) \text{ DNE, and } c \in \text{dom}(f)
cheatSheet.quickCheck: Always verify critical points are in the domain of the original function

masteryCriteria.recognitionSpeed: < 5 seconds to identify critical point problem
selfCheckQuestions: Did I check where f'(x) is undefined?
Are my critical points in the domain of f?
